title: West Bengal elections: Ahead of counting, TMC & BJP spar over EVM security
content: Ahead of Bengal's vote counting, Trinamool and BJP traded accusations over EVM strongroom security. TMC alleged CCTV blackouts in North 24 Parganas, while BJP pointed to alleged unauthorized access and attempted breaches in Salt Lake and Burdwan. Both parties raised concerns about the integrity of the storage facilities.

title: Bargi dam tragedy: MP tourism launched cruise despite high-wind forecast
content: Tragedy struck at Bargi Dam as a cruise, reportedly launched despite severe wind warnings, claimed 11 lives, including children. Officials revealed forecasts predicted winds far exceeding safety limits for water sports. While other water activities in the state were suspended due to similar alerts, this operation proceeded, sparking criticism and allegations of neglect within the tourism department.

title: In Gujarat, bouncers hired to watch screen addicts
content: Desperate parents in Gujarat are hiring bouncers to manage teenagers battling severe phone, gaming, and drug addictions.  These security personnel, costing up to Rs 65,000 monthly, are deployed 24/7 to prevent violence, substance abuse, and risky behavior, highlighting a growing trend of extreme de-addiction measures.

title: Ruturaj Gaikwad masterclass sinks MI as CSK keep playoff hopes alive
content: Chennai Super Kings secured a vital eight-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians, bolstered by a disciplined bowling attack led by Noor Ahmad and Anshul Kamboj. Ruturaj Gaikwad's unbeaten 67 anchored the chase, with strong support from Kartik Sharma, ensuring CSK's playoff hopes remain alive while pushing MI further down the table.

title: Snap AI coding shift: Evan Spiegel says Claude now writes most new code
content: Snap CEO Evan Spiegel is impressed by AI's rapid advancement, noting tools like Anthropic's Claude are transforming software development. He predicts companies will reallocate resources from engineering to distribution as AI automates coding, with over two-thirds of new code at Snap now written by AI systems.

title: India successfully conducts 2nd test of long-range hypersonic anti-ship missile
content: India has successfully tested its long-range hypersonic anti-ship missile (LR-AShM), boasting a strike range exceeding 1,500 km and speeds of Mach 10. This advanced weapon, capable of evading modern defenses and engaging large targets, significantly enhances India's maritime capabilities. The successful flight test marks a major stride in indigenous defense innovation, bolstering national security and self-reliance.

title: CBI arrests 2 for Rs 2,700 crore fraud after extradition from UAE
content: India successfully repatriated two fugitives, Kamlesh Parekh and Aalok Kumar, from the UAE. Parekh, involved in a Rs 2,672 crore banking fraud, was brought to Kolkata. Kumar, a key figure in a passport forgery racket, was handed over to Haryana police in Mumbai. Both were apprehended following Interpol Red Notices.

title: Jaipur court grants divorce, cites wife’s social media conduct as ‘mental cruelty’
content: A Jaipur family court granted a man a divorce, citing his wife's objectionable social media activity as mental cruelty. The court ruled that sharing compromising photos online caused the husband significant emotional distress and damaged his reputation. This ruling underscores how online behavior can be crucial evidence in matrimonial disputes, leading to the dissolution of the decade-old marriage.

title: CEO says ‘When Google chief agrees with Elon, everyone must pay attention’; Musk responds
content: Elon Musk agreed with a post highlighting Google CEO Sundar Pichai's vision of space-based data centers becoming "the new normal" within a decade. This aligns with Musk's own long-held views on utilizing space for large-scale infrastructure. Pichai previously mentioned Google's "moon shot" to place data centers in orbit to harness solar energy, with a first step planned for 2027.

title: Sudesh Kumar, hero of ‘Saranga’ and producer of ‘Uljhan’, passes away
content: Veteran actor and producer Sudesh Kumar, known for his roles in 'Saranga' and 'Chhoti Bahen', passed away at 95 in Mumbai. Kumar, who also produced thrillers like 'Uljhan', began his career in theatre before making a mark in films. His wife confirmed his passing after a brief illness. He was born in Peshawar and later moved to Bombay.

title: West Bengal elections: Both central, state officers at counting centres, EC tells SC
content: The Supreme Court dismissed a Trinamool Congress plea challenging the Election Commission's deployment of counting staff. The EC assured the court that both central and state government employees would be assigned to counting tables to ensure balance, refuting claims of bias. The court accepted the EC's assurance and dismissed the petition.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ mints over Rs 1785 cr as ‘Raja Shivaji’ takes lead on Friday
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its steady box office run, grossing over Rs 1785 crore globally after 44 days. Despite facing new competition, including Riteish Deshmukh's 'Raja Shivaji' which took the lead on Friday, 'Dhurandhar 2' added Rs 1.05 crore on its 7th Friday, bringing its India net collection to Rs 1,136.14 crore.

title: ‘I am vegetarian so need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: Two FIRs against defector MP Pathak? Punjab cops won’t say
content: Two FIRs, reportedly under non-bailable charges, have been filed against Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak in Punjab, shortly after he switched from AAP to BJP. Political leaders from BJP and SAD have decried the move as "political vendetta" and an attempt to intimidate party members. Security was heightened at Pathak's Delhi residence.

title: Rohit Jain appointed as RBI deputy governor
content: Rohit Jain, an executive director with three decades of experience at the Reserve Bank of India, has been appointed as Deputy Governor. His three-year term begins on or after May 3, 2026, succeeding T Rabi Sankar. Jain, who previously oversaw banking supervision, joins SC Murmu as an internal promotion to the DG post, with departments likely to be reshuffled.

title: Election Commission releases final data: 4.88 crore people voted in Tamil Nadu
content: Tamil Nadu witnessed a record 85.1% voter turnout in the recent assembly elections, with 4.88 crore citizens casting their ballots. Women voters significantly outnumbered men, continuing a trend from previous years. The Election Commission faced delays in releasing data but assured robust security and transparency for the upcoming vote counting on May 4.

title: Laura Loomer warns Candace Owens of lawsuit in Erika Kirk row
content: Candace Owens is facing a potential lawsuit threat from Laura Loomer after refusing to retract claims involving Erika Kirk and the 2025 killing of Charlie Kirk. Owens insists she stands by her statements, while Loomer warned legal action is imminent. The dispute has intensified with a separate lawsuit filed by Brian Harpole, escalating tensions.

title: Vrabel marriage on brink as counselling fails amid Russini drama
content: Mike Vrabel and Jen Vrabel are reportedly undergoing marriage counselling as fallout from the Dianna Russini controversy strains their relationship. Sources say Vrabel is dealing with intense emotions and focusing on work while trying to repair the situation. Jen feels embarrassed but hopes to save the marriage. Despite efforts to reconcile, insiders suggest the situation may not be resolved quickly.

title: Maria Sharapova net worth 2026: Grand Slam icon’s empire grows
content: Maria Sharapova, a tennis icon, amassed an estimated $180 million net worth primarily through lucrative endorsement deals and her candy brand, Sugarpova. Her remarkable career included winning all four Grand Slams and reaching world No. 1. After retiring in 2020, she continues to thrive in business and investments, maintaining her global recognition.

title: Government notifies FDI changes on China funds
content: India has eased foreign investment rules, allowing up to 100% FDI in insurance and relaxing norms for investments from neighbouring countries. Companies with up to 10% Chinese holding can now invest via the automatic route, though entities registered in China itself are excluded. These changes aim to boost capital inflows amidst a weakening rupee.

title: West Bengal Elections: Exit polls for stock market, TMC to get 200+, says Didi
content: Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee dismissed exit poll predictions, asserting Trinamool's victory with over 200 seats and accusing the BJP of manipulating markets. She urged counting agents to remain vigilant and not panic over initial postal ballot results. Meanwhile, opposition leader Suvendu Adhikari called for peace and acceptance of the mandate, warning against post-poll violence.

title: Prasoon Joshi appointed as Prasar Bharati chairman
content: Prasoon Joshi, a renowned lyricist and communications expert, has been appointed as the new chairman of Prasar Bharati, filling a crucial leadership gap. His extensive experience in advertising, cinema, and public communication is expected to bring fresh energy and a creative voice to the public broadcaster as it embraces digital transformation and evolving audience tastes.

title: Kerala verdict: Pinarayi Vijayan's welfare rule - will it fare well or get a farewell?
content: Kerala's upcoming election sees the LDF government banking on its development model and welfare gains for a third consecutive term, a feat rarely achieved due to anti-incumbency. Despite high SDG rankings, the state faces challenges in gender equality and economic growth, while migration and wage gaps emerge as key voter concerns.

title: Why the biggest ‘winners’ in these assembly elections may not even be political parties
content: Millions are at stake on Polymarket, a prediction platform, as Indian assembly election results loom.  Over $20 million is tied to Tamil Nadu's outcome alone, with significant bets also placed on West Bengal, Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry.  Despite Indian law prohibiting such markets, users leverage crypto and VPNs to participate, showcasing a growing trend in crowd-sourced forecasting.

title: Niti Aayog revamp: Centre names 2 academics; PM cites ‘rich experience’
content: Niti Aayog welcomes academics Joram Aniya and R Balasubramaniam as new members, bolstering policy-making with their extensive experience. Prime Minister Modi lauded their contributions, anticipating innovation and growth.

title: West Bengal polls: EC scraps Falta election, orders repolling on May 21
content: Bengal's electoral landscape has shifted as the Election Commission cancelled polling in Falta constituency due to widespread EVM tampering. A repoll is set for May 21, making it a unique post-result election. Allegations of threats against villagers and the arrest of a candidate's aides have heightened tensions, focusing attention on the remaining seats to decide the state's next government.

title: Oil’s well: Venezuela’s crude exports hit highest since 2018 — India, US and other buyers line up
content: Venezuela's crude exports surged 14% to 1.23 million barrels per day in April, reaching their fastest pace in over seven years. This increase, driven by demand from the US, India, and Europe, follows a US agreement that softened sanctions, allowing major trading firms and partners to resume lifting Venezuelan crude.

title: 'Harassed by wife, sister-in-law': 30-yr-old judge dies by suicide at Delhi home
content: A 30-year-old judicial officer, Aman Kumar Sharma, died by suicide in Delhi, his family alleging harassment by his wife, also a judicial officer, and her IAS sister. Sharma had reportedly expressed distress to his father, citing marital conflicts and interference. Police are investigating the circumstances, including the family's serious allegations.

title: 'Not the season': Hardik Pandya expresses discontent after CSK beat MI
content: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya admitted “not the season” after MI suffered an eight-wicket loss to Chennai Super Kings in IPL 2026. MI managed only 159/7 despite Naman Dhir’s 57, while Noor Ahmad and Anshul Kamboj starred with the ball for CSK. Ruturaj Gaikwad’s unbeaten 67 and Kartik Sharma’s 54 not out guided Chennai to victory, further hurting Mumbai’s fading playoff hopes.

title: New propulsion system to boost subs’ staying power under water almost ready: DRDO chief
content: India's Navy is set for a significant boost with DRDO's new air-independent propulsion system for submarines, dramatically extending submerged endurance.  A land-based prototype was successfully tested, and the full system is nearing completion for installation on the 14th submarine. This development, along with advanced missile and weapon systems, enhances national defense capabilities.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for Sunday
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 3, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: ‘We are keeping it simple’- Gaikwad eyes playoff push after big win vs MI
content: Ruturaj Gaikwad led Chennai Super Kings to a crucial eight-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians, keeping their playoff hopes alive.  His unbeaten 67, coupled with Anshul Kamboj's three-wicket haul, was pivotal. Gaikwad praised the team's resilience and the bowlers' ability to regain control.  The win underscores CSK's determined approach to the tournament's business end.

title: J&K: Warming Gulmarg turns to tech for four-season skiing future
content: Gulmarg's ski season faces an uncertain future due to shrinking winters and erratic snowfall. Authorities are fast-tracking plans for artificial snow-making systems and synthetic slopes to ensure the resort's viability. This initiative aims to reduce reliance on natural snow and extend skiing into a four-season activity, safeguarding its legacy.

title: Jammu bridge collapse toll rises to three, 2 engineers suspended
content: Tragedy struck in Jammu's Thuther area as a collapsed bridge during repair work claimed three lives. Rescue operations recovered the bodies after a section of the structure gave way. Authorities have launched a probe, suspending two engineers and forming a committee to investigate the incident and prevent future mishaps. One worker and a rescuer were also injured.

title: War and your SIPs: Stay, pause or exit?
content: Indian equities experienced significant volatility as geopolitical tensions impacted markets, leading to a weaker rupee and investor caution. While fear drives knee-jerk reactions, the core design of Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) is built to navigate these phases, turning market swings into a potential long-term advantage for disciplined investors.

title: Lifeline wasted! Samson falls to Bumrah just balls after Jacks’ blunder
content: Jasprit Bumrah's precision proved too much for Sanju Samson, who was dismissed for 11 after a dropped catch on 6. Bumrah's relentless bowling in the second over, despite the early reprieve, ultimately led to Samson's edge being safely taken. This crucial early wicket kept Mumbai Indians in control of the chase, setting the tone for their pursuit of 159.

title: R Madhavan warns of life after 60: Dependence is worse than hell
content: R Madhavan has opened up about ageing, life planning and the challenges of staying relevant in a rapidly changing world. Speaking about his current work and personal reflections, the actor emphasised that while people are living longer today, very few are truly prepared for what comes after retirement.

title: World laughter day episode: Samay Raina, Sunil Pal trade jabs
content: In a side-splitting special of The Great Indian Kapil Show, comedians Samay Raina and Ranveer Allahbadia took center stage to tackle their recent controversy surrounding India's Got Latent. The atmosphere shifted from light-hearted to charged when the surprise entry of Sunil Pal, a familiar rival of Samay, turned the tables.

title: 'It was very difficult': Harmanpreet reacts to key absence in T20 WC squad
content: India captain Harmanpreet Kaur admitted Amanjot Kaur’s injury has left a major gap in the squad ahead of the Women’s T20 World Cup but backed Bharti Fulmali and Radha Yadav to step up. Amanjot is out for four to five months with a back injury. Harmanpreet also defended India’s bowlers after the South Africa series loss and said the upcoming camp and England series would help the team regain rhythm before the World Cup.

title: Roots and Rupture: Trump withdraws forces from land of his forebears after clash with Merz
content: US President Trump ordered the withdrawal of approximately 5,000 US troops from Germany following a public dispute with Chancellor Merz over the war in Iran. This move, seen as punitive, signals a growing rift in the transatlantic alliance and raises questions about US commitment to NATO, despite Trump's German heritage.

title: The great 'gas-by': 45,000-tonne LPG tanker 'Sarv Shakti' crosses Hormuz
content: An India-linked supertanker, Sarv Shakti, has successfully crossed the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy corridor under intense pressure due to Middle East tensions. This passage marks a significant breakthrough for India, the world's second-largest LPG consumer, as it secures essential cooking fuel amid disrupted supply chains and a US blockade on Iran-linked shipping.

title: 'Will reward those who faced CAPF brutality': Mamata addresses counting agents
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee expressed confidence in the Trinamool Congress securing over 200 seats, dismissing exit polls as stock market manipulation. She vowed to reward TMC workers who faced alleged brutality from Central Armed Police Forces. Repolling in South 24 Parganas district saw significant voter turnout amidst tight security.

title: Court denies bail to woman accused in TCS Nashik case
content: The accused is now likely to appeal against the decision in the Bombay High Court. Four others arrested in the case have been remanded to police custody till May 5

title: Rahul Gandhi cites Gujarat BJP chief’s ‘indecent remarks’ against Geniben Thakor
content: Mr. Gandhi says the controversy exposes the BJP’s anti-women and Manuwadi mindset and questions the party’s commitment to empowering women

title: Ernakulam sees heavy inflow of suspected synthetic drugs
content: The spike follows a crackdown triggered by a raid at a star hotel at Kadavanthra in March, where eight professionals were arrested with suspected narcotic substances

title: 83.96% men, 86.2% women voted at polling stations in Assembly poll across Tamil Nadu: EC
content: A total of 4,87,98,833 voters out of 5,73,43,291 turned up to exercise their franchise on April 23 in T.N. Assembly polls; total votes polled may go up as the current figures do not include postal ballots or senior citizens who voted through Form 12D

title: Assembly elections: ECI orders another round of repoll in all booths of Bengal's Falta seat on May 21 | Highlights
content: Demanding safety after counting of votes, hundreds of villagers staged a protest in Falta in West Bengal’s South 24 Parganas district, alleging they were receiving life threats from TMC workers in the area

title: ECI announces repolling for Falta Assembly seat in West Bengal on May 21
content: In a notification, the ECI says multiple complaints of electoral malpractices were received on the day of polling in the constituency. The votes will be counted on May 24

title: West Bengal election: Hundreds stage protest over ‘threats’ by TMC workers in Falta
content: The CRPF deputy commandant visited the area with a large force and assured the demonstrators of ensuring their safety.

title: India at Venice Biennale 2026 curtain raiser: Many voices, one resonance
content: From soil and thread to immersive myth and monumental sculpture, India’s Venice Biennale 2026 presence expands beyond the pavilion into a layered meditation on home and identity

title: EC makes final preparations for counting of votes at 62 centres across Tamil Nadu
content: Chief Electoral Officer Archana Patnaik holds review meeting with with Election Commission officials ahead of counting day on May 4

title: Tele-MANAS helpline counselling 3,000 people per month in A.P., says Health Minister
content: Tele-MANAS centres in Vijayawada and Visakhapatnam, which were opened in 2022 and 2023 respectively, have received 67,573 calls so far

title: Aqua farmers in upbeat mood as India’s seafood exports cross ₹72,000-crore mark
content: Farmers request the Centre to have a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) with more countries to reduce the dependence on the U.S.A.

title: Renowned lyricist Prasoon Joshi appointed Prasar Bharati Chairman
content: Joshi had earlier served as the Chairperson of the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC), Mumbai, CEO of McCann World Group India, and Chairman of McCann World Group Asia Pacific

title: Four policemen killed as gunpowder recovered from Maoist hideout explodes
content: Gunpowder stashed in sacks may have exploded due to high temperature and chemical reaction, say police; three victims die on the spot, one during treatment; incident happened during de-mining operation

title: Tea garden workers union moves ILO seeking restoration of their rights in West Bengal
content: PBCMS invokes Article 24 jurisdiction of the ILO which allows employers’ or workers’ organisations to make a representation against any member State, alleging ineffective observance of a ratified Convention

title: NGT order warns of fines to southern States if clean air funds are not utilised
content: The tribunal has ordered all five southern States and the Union Territory of Puducherry to ensure time-bound implementation of their State Action Plans; these directions assume significance as it puts the spotlight on air pollution governance in the southern States — an issue that has largely been dominated by the northern regions, particularly Delhi-NCR

title: Raghav Chadha | The prodigy who walked away
content: The Rajya Sabha MP, who was one of the close confidants of Kejriwal, now says the ‘toxic work culture’ of AAP forced him to quit the party and join the BJP

title: INDIA bloc likely to meet after poll results to discuss strategy on delimitation, women’s quota
content: Post Assembly election results in May, the Opposition bloc mulls meeting to brainstorm future strategy on key issues tied up with the expansion of Lok Sabha

title: IMD forecasts more heatwaves in coastal Andhra Pradesh, warmer nights across State
content: Slight relief likely in north but above-normal minimum temperatures to persist across State despite 33.5% to 45% probability of rain

title: Watch: How Kerala, West Bengal and Assam voted in 2021
content: With voting over and counting set for May 4, this explainer looks at how Kerala, West Bengal and Assam voted in 2021, and what those patterns could tell us about the results this time.

title: Jabalpur cruise tragedy: Death toll rises to 11 as bodies of two kids found; search continues
content: Two more missing people from the capsized cruise boat are yet to be found; M.P. Tourism Secretary says a revised SOP will be prepared to prevent such incidents in future

title: College admissions: Demand for specialised, industry-oriented programmes over traditional degrees
content: Commerce remains the most sought-after stream, particularly courses integrated with data analytics and finance

title: Election Commission appoints 165 additional counting observers, 77 police observers for counting votes in West Bengal
content: Both the Bharatiya Janata Party and the Trinamool Congress hailed the Supreme Court directions on May 2 on the Election Commission’s circular over the deployment of counting personnel for West Bengal

title: Maniyar hydel project: KSEB’s takeover bid put on hold
content: Recently, govt. asked KSEB to take over the 12 MW unit since the pact with Carborundum had ended in December 2024. On April 29, signages proclaiming ‘KSEB property’ were planted on the unit premises, prompting criticism from the company. On Saturday, the govt. decides to freeze the move till a panel decides on the company’s application to extend the pact.

title: What is India’s new Cell Broadcast System? Everything you need to know
content: The alert system was sent across the network of all telecom operators across the country on Saturday, except in poll-bound States

title: High turnout for repoll in 15 West Bengal booths; peaceful amid tight security
content: Protests erupt in Falta over BJP’s allegations of malpractice and voter harassment, with a decision on repolls in the seat still pending

title: One-man panel on TTD laddu row finds lapses, suggests reforms for Trust Board
content: The panel report suggests a reduction in the number of Trust Board members, changes in their eligibility criteria, strengthening of the vigilance wing and a manual for procurement, storage, quality and inspection

title: Bishnoi gang shoots at restaurant next to Kap's cafe in Canada; issues warning to Kapil Sharma
content: . Raj Shekhar Jha is a journalist for the Times of India with over a decade of experience in reporting on national security, terrorism, crime and prisons.

title: Analysis: Tamil Nadu Heads For Dravidian Shakeout After Assembly Poll Verdict
content: The only word that might connect all these forces competing today but shifting loyalties tomorrow in an uneasy coalition is the adjective"Dravidian".

title: Oil Tanker Hijacked Off Yemen Coast, Directed Towards Somalia
content: Unidentified attackers hijacked an oil tanker on Saturday off the coast of Yemen in the Gulf of Aden and directed it towards Somalia, the Yemeni coast guard said.

title: Operation Sindoor Reflected India's Resolve To Eliminate Terrorism: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Saturday described Operation Sindoor as a reflection of the Modi government's "unwavering resolve" to eliminate terrorism through "decisive action".

title: Exclusive: New Drone Built By IIT Jammu Can't Be 'Jammed Or Detected'
content: Unlike others, the drone developed by IIT Jammu students evades detection by ditching traditional radio frequencies using advanced technology.

title: "Subversion Of Democratic Process": Bengal's Falta To See Repolling On May 21
content: Poll body announces repolling in all 285 polling stations in West Bengal's Falta, cites "subversion of democratic process"

title: India's Latest Nuclear Reactor Win Can Lead To 700 Years Of Energy Security
content: India has achieved one of the most consequential milestones in its atomic energy journey, as the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) achieved criticality.

title: Assam Opposition Dismisses Exit Poll Predictions Ahead Of Counting Of Votes
content: With the counting of votes for the Assam Assembly elections just days away, the Congress-led opposition alliance on Saturday projected confidence in securing power in the state, brushing aside exit poll forecasts that favour the ruling BJP-led allian

title: "Bloodbath": Dutch Princesses Targeted In Chilling 'Nazi' Murder Plot
content: The suspect, who faces a preliminary court hearing on Monday morning, was found with two axes engraved with "Alexia", "Mossad" and Sieg Heil, which is a verbal salute used by Nazis.

title: Lapses, Remorse, 'Act Of Nature' Claim: Cruise Boat Pilot's Big Reveal
content: Mahesh Patel said he requested the hotel's reception desk to send a rescue boat as the accident was imminent. In the next sentence, he revealed a major security lapse.

title: "Central Staff, Give Credence": Top Court To Trinamool Over Plea Against Poll Body
content: "They're Government Staff, Give Them Some Credence": Supreme Court To Trinamool Over Petition Against Election Commission

title: Who Is Sagar Adani, And Why He Matters to the Global Energy Transition
content: Sagar Adani, 31, is an executive director at Adani Green Energy, who now sits at the centre of one of India's most ambitious energy expansion plans.

title: 'Uniform For Service': Mumbai Woman Who Went Viral For Shouting During Rally
content: Teena Choudhry in her video released on Saturday further urged political parties to not use her as a "shoulder to take aim or potshots at anybody".

title: "Frustrated Statements": Assam BJP Slams Congress Over Strongroom Claims
content: Referring to the Opposition's concerns over strongroom security, Assam BJP Chief Spokesperson Kishore Upadhyay questioned whether similar allegations would be raised in states where the INDIA alliance is expected to perform well.

title: 'Fully Prepared For American Foolishness': Iran Warns Of Possible Renewed War
content: According to Iran's Fars News, Mohammad Jafar Asadi, a senior figure in the Iranian military's central command, said, "a renewed conflict between Iran and the United States is likely."

title: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Voting Live Updates: 90% Voter Turnout In Magrahat Paschim, Diamond Harbour Repolls
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Voting Live Updates: The repolls were ordered in 11 polling stations of the Magrahat Paschim assembly constituency and four in Diamond Harbour.

title: 4 Children Found Dead Inside UP House, Mother Missing: Cops
content: Four children were allegedly bludgeoned to death inside their house in Uttar Pradesh's Ambedkar Nagar district, while their mother is reportedly missing, police said on Saturday.
